text:
Market today: Sentiment is cautiously bullish - Wells Fargo calling a summer rally and chatter that the AI trade could leg higher are tailwinds for exactly your kind of book. Tech and chips are the focal point; that narrative directly lifts your heaviest names.

Your holdings: SOXL (-7.06%) is the one screaming for attention - it's a 3x leveraged chip ETF sitting 15% above its $200 stop, but leverage decay grinds you even in sideways chop, and on margin that double-leverage effect is real. SPCX (+0.53%) is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history - margin amplifies any gap-down hard. SMH (-0.69%) is mild but worth watching as the unleveraged chip read.

Watch: (1) Whether SOXL holds above $200 stop on any dip - a flush through there on margin is a serious hit. (2) Any concrete AI spending news that could validate the 'AI trade powering higher' headline.

Bottom line: Your book is set up well for a summer tech rally, but SOXL's leverage decay and your margin exposure make stop discipline the only job that matters right now.
plays: SOXL: Stop is $200 - do not move it lower to give it room. At -7% with 3x leverage on margin, a bad day compounds fast; know your exit.
CRWD: Already up 3.26%; scanner shows Grade A long setup with entry 741.42-742.17, stop 740.31. Fine to hold or add a small unit only if it holds above that entry band.
No new buys beyond that - AI rally talk is not confirmed catalyst enough to chase. Hold, watch stops.
